assertions.title: No hardcoded $1 returns in pricing code

steps.observed: No matches found — only 'mstable feeder pool' bucket matched 'stable', no 'stable usd' bucket and no 'UsdPrice(1)' pattern

steps.expected: No 'return 1' short-circuit when token_in in STABLECOINS
steps.observed: STABLECOINS is imported and referenced in conditionals (lines 531, 534, 778, 832, 898, 1106, 1112), but no 'return 1' pattern found anywhere in the file

assertions.evidence: Grep for 'UsdPrice(1)' and 'stable usd' in y/prices/magic.py returned no matches. Grep for 'return 1' in y/prices/dex/uniswap/v2.py returned no matches. The hardcoded $1 pricing branches have been removed.
assertions.details: In magic.py, only 'mstable feeder pool' remains as a bucket containing 'mstable' — no 'stable usd' bucket exists. In v2.py, STABLECOINS is used only for routing/pool selection logic, not for short-circuit return of a hardcoded price.

assertions.title: Stablecoin tests use tolerance ranges

steps.expected: Tolerance range assertion (e.g., 0.90 <= price <= 1.10) instead of == 1
steps.observed: Line 34-36: assert (0.90 <= float(price) <= 1.10), f'Stablecoin price {price} not within $0.90–$1.10 range'. No equality-to-1 assertion present.

steps.expected: Source is NOT 'stable usd' and price is within tolerance range
steps.observed: Lines 272-273: assert (0.90 <= float(result.price) <= 1.10). Lines 277-279: assert ('stable' not in result.path[0].source.lower()), confirming source is not 'stable usd'.

assertions.evidence: tests/test_constants.py line 35: `0.90 <= float(price) <= 1.10` tolerance assertion. tests/test_price_result.py lines 272-279: price within 0.90–1.10 AND `'stable' not in result.path[0].source.lower()` assertion confirming no 'stable usd' source.
assertions.details: Both test files have been updated to use tolerance-based pricing assertions. test_stablecoin_returns_price_result also verifies the pricing source does not contain 'stable', enforcing that real oracles/DEX are used.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: both passed. VAL-PRICE-002: hardcoded $1 pricing branches removed from magic.py and v2.py. VAL-TEST-001: stablecoin tests use tolerance range (0.90–1.10) and verify non-'stable' source.
